learn.title: Pythagoras Table
learn.hoverHint: Hover any cell to highlight its row and column.

hints.title: The 9s Trick
hints.description: Multiply by 10 and subtract the number itself. Example: 9 × 7 = (10 × 7) - 7 = 63.

hints.title: Secret of 5s
hints.description: Multiply by 10 and divide by half. Example: 5 × 6 = 60 / 2 = 30.

hints.title: Multiplying by 4
hints.description: Double the number, then double it again! Example: 4 × 6 -> double 6 is 12, double 12 is 24.

hints.title: Magic of 11 (from 1 to 9)
hints.description: Just write the digit twice! Example: 11 × 4 = 44, 11 × 7 = 77.

funFacts: Zero (0) is the only number that cannot be represented in Roman numerals.
If you multiply any number by 9, the sum of the digits in the answer will always be 9.
The number 7 is the most popular 'favorite number' in the world.
Four (4) is the only number in the English language that has the same number of letters as its value.
The word 'hundred' comes from the old Norse word 'hundrath', which actually meant 120, not 100!
2 is the only even prime number in mathematics.
Bees build their honeycombs in the shape of perfect hexagons.
A 'Googol' is the number 1 followed by 100 zeros. This is where the company 'Google' got its name.
2520 is the smallest number that can be exactly divided by all the numbers from 1 to 10.
A 'jiffy' is an actual unit of time. It is exactly one-hundredth of a second.
You can cut a perfectly round pizza into 8 slices using only 3 straight cuts.
While 13 is considered unlucky in many countries, it is a lucky number in Italy.
Some species of cicadas live underground for exactly 13 or 17 years. Both are prime numbers!
The seeds in the center of a sunflower follow the mathematical Fibonacci sequence.
A 'palindrome' number reads the same forwards and backwards, like 1001 or 3443.
In ancient Babylon, people counted using a base of 60 instead of 10. That's why we have 60 seconds in a minute.
Every single odd number in the English language contains the letter 'e' (one, three, five...).
If you add up all the numbers from 1 to 100, the answer is 5050.
There are 'Vampire numbers' in math! For example, 1260 = 21 × 60. The digits in the answer are the same as the problem.
In the Georgian language, counting is based on 20, because ancient people used both fingers and toes to count.
The equals sign (=) was invented in 1557 by Robert Recorde because he was tired of writing the word 'equals'.
In Thai, the number 5 is pronounced as 'Ha'. So '555' is used to represent laughter (Hahaha).
Zero was discovered in India and brought to Europe by the Arabs. Before that, Europeans did not use 0!
A standard deck of cards has 52 cards, which matches the 52 weeks in a year.
If you put one grain of rice on each chessboard square, doubling each time, you get an astronomically huge number.
The angles inside any triangle always add up to 180 degrees.
A square has exactly 4 lines of symmetry.
A full circle contains 360 degrees.
Pi (pi) is an endless decimal that never repeats in a fixed pattern.
At exactly 3 o'clock, the clock hands make a right angle.
A leap year has 366 days instead of 365.
Computers use binary, a number system made only of 0 and 1.
A Sudoku puzzle is a 9 by 9 logic grid.
In a standard domino set, opposite ends can create patterns based on number pairs.
A pentagon has 5 sides.
Regular hexagons tile perfectly without gaps.
Every prime number greater than 2 is odd.
The smallest perfect number is 6, because 1 + 2 + 3 = 6.
A dozen means 12, and a gross means 144.
A stop sign is shaped like an octagon with 8 sides.
The tangram puzzle uses exactly 7 geometric pieces.
A Rubik's Cube has more than 43 quintillion possible arrangements.
The abacus is one of the oldest counting tools in the world.
Zero factorial is 1, written as 0! = 1.
The number 1 is special, but it is not a prime number.
There are exactly 1000 meters in one kilometer.
The word percent means 'per hundred'.
On the Celsius scale, water freezes at 0 degrees.
Pythagoras' theorem connects the sides of right triangles.
The area of a rectangle is length multiplied by width.
Perimeter means the total distance around a shape.
A cube has 6 faces, 8 vertices, and 12 edges.
On a standard die, opposite faces always add up to 7.
The chance of flipping heads on a fair coin is 1 out of 2.
Two coin flips create 4 equally likely outcomes.
24 hours equals 1440 minutes.
60 minutes equals 3600 seconds.
A year is about 365.24 days long, which is why leap years exist.
Planet orbits are usually ellipses, not perfect circles.
The golden ratio appears in art, nature, and design.
Fractals are patterns that repeat similar shapes at different sizes.
Pascal's triangle hides many number patterns.
2 to the 10th power equals 1024.
One million has 6 zeros.
One billion has 9 zeros.
In Roman numerals, M means 1000.
An acute angle is smaller than 90 degrees.
An obtuse angle is bigger than 90 but smaller than 180 degrees.
A straight angle measures exactly 180 degrees.
A right angle measures exactly 90 degrees.
One full turn is 360 degrees.
The coordinate plane uses x and y to locate points.
The origin is the point (0, 0) on a graph.
Absolute value tells how far a number is from zero.
Butterfly wings often show mirror symmetry.
Barcodes store numbers using patterns of dark and light lines.
QR codes use square patterns to store lots of data.
GPS uses math and timing to estimate your location.
Map scales are ratios that compare real distance to map distance.
Triangles, squares, and hexagons can tessellate in many patterns.
The Fibonacci sequence starts 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8 and keeps growing.
Many shells and plants form spirals that follow math patterns.
The chance of rolling a 6 on a fair die is 1 out of 6.
Mean is another word for average.
Median is the middle number in an ordered list.
Mode is the value that appears most often.
Zero is the additive identity because n + 0 = n.
One is the multiplicative identity because n x 1 = n.
Any number multiplied by 0 equals 0.
Order of operations helps everyone solve expressions the same way.
Parentheses are solved first in arithmetic expressions.
Even numbers end with 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8.
Odd numbers end with 1, 3, 5, 7, or 9.
A centimeter is 10 millimeters.
A meter is 100 centimeters.
A right triangle always has one 90-degree angle.
The sum of two even numbers is always even.
The sum of two odd numbers is always even.
A heptagon is a polygon with 7 sides.
